title: Prevention of Clostridium perfringens infection - Canada.ca
content:
## Prevention of Clostridium perfringens infection
Learn how Clostridium perfringens infections can be prevented.
- Learn how Clostridium perfringens infections can be prevented
- For more information

## Learn how Clostridium perfringens infections can be prevented
Foods that contain the bacteria usually look, smell, and taste normal.
You can lower your risk of infection and other food-related illnesses by:
- following general food safety tips, including proper: cooking and reheating of food to recommended temperatures food storage microwave use handling of leftovers
- cooking and reheating of food to recommended temperatures
- handling of leftovers
You can help prevent others from getting sick by contacting your local public health authority to report concerns about establishments serving food.

## For more information
- Public health notices
- General food safety tips
- Report a food safety or labelling concern
